body.description: End‑to‑end fresh message creation, signing, validation, delivery, processing and summarization
steps: deliver via create‑signed‑message to target inbox
process by outbox‑write‑guard and schema validator

findings.note: Signature uses current key_id; historic snapshots still contain old key_id entries which can cause verification fallback to legacy paths if not cleaned.
Verifier falls back to legacy `verifyAgainstTrustStore` only when `testMode` is true; in production any mismatch aborts, preventing silent failures.
Delivery script writes directly to target inbox; no intermediate staging directory, eliminating hidden path risks.
Outbox guard enforces `content_hash` and `signature` fields; old messages lacking these would be rejected, but legacy logs still contain such entries.

recommendations: Purge legacy messages lacking signatures from inboxes
Align all snapshot JWS to current key_id
Add automated regression test for message lifecycle across lanes
